zh.lifeSummary: 當María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira於1189年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德蒙特亞利格 德 甘波斯蒙特亞利格時，她的父親João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima"19歲，母親María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña23歲。她和Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於大約1246年在她的家鄉享年58歲。

nameMeanings.name: Yáñez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Francisco, Carlos, Jesus, Manuel, Raul, Luis, Miguel, Rafael, Roberto, Mario.
Spanish (Yáñez): patronymic from a variant of the personal name Juan, an equivalent of John . Compare Anez , Ibanez , and Yanes .

variants.name: Yanes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Pedro, Armando, Felipe, Francisco, Manuel, Margarito, Mario, Miguel.
Spanish: variant of Yáñez (see Yanez ).
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian (of Slovenian origin) Janeš and also of Slovenian Janež (see Janes ).

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Seanez
meanings: Hispanic (also Seáñez): perhaps a cognate of Spanish Yáñez (see Yanez ). Compare Sianez .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lima
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Mario, Luis, Juan, Raul, Ricardo, Roberto, Eduardo, Francisco, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Duarte, Guilherme, Vasco, Joaquim, Albano, Catarina, Goncalo, Margarida, Terezinha.
Spanish: possibly from lima ‘file’.
Portuguese: topographic name for someone living on the banks of the river of this name (of pre-Roman origin, probably akin to a Celtic element lemos, limos ‘elm’).

variants.name: Limas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Manuel, Juan, Lupe, Raul, Ricardo, Ruben, Fernando, Francisco, Luis, Maria Del Rosario.
Portuguese and Spanish: variant of   Lima  .

nameMeanings.name: Ribeira
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Galician and Portuguese: variant of Ribeira, a habitational name from any of numerous places called Ribeira, meaning ‘bank, shore’.

nameMeanings.name: María
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Maria
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira was born in 1189, in Montealegre, Montealegre de Campos, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spain, her father, João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima", was 19 and her mother, María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña, was 23. She had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses. She died about 1246, in her hometown, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira est née en 1189, à Montealegre, Montealegre de Campos, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima", avait 19 ans et sa mère, María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña, avait 23 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses. Elle est décédée environ 1246, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira 1189 in Montealegre, Montealegre de Campos, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima" 19 und ihre Mutter María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña 23. Sie hatte mit Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1246 im Alter von 58 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira è nata il nel 1189 nel Montealegre, Montealegre de Campos, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spagna, sua padre, João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima", aveva 19 anni e sua madre, María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña, aveva 23 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses. Morì circa 1246, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 58 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira(이)가 1189년 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 발라도리드 몽올그르 데 캄푸스 몽올그르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima"(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1246년 그녀의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira nasceu em 1189, em Montealegre, Montealegre de Campos, Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima", tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña, tinha 23 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas com Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1246, em sua cidade natal, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira родилась в 1189 г. в местности монтилегр, монтилегр де кампос, валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания, её отцу по имени João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima" было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña, 23. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses. Она умерла около 1246 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando María Yáñez de Lima y Ribeira nació en 1189, en Montealegre, Montealegre de Campos, Valladolid, Castilla y León, España, su papá, João Fernandes "o Bom de Lima", tenía 19 años y su mamá, María Páez de Ribeira La Ribeiriña, tenía 23 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas con Alfonso Tellez de Meneses IV Señor de Meneses. Murió aproximadamente 1246, en su pueblo, a la edad de 58 años.
